@etirabys Had terrible anxiety based insomnia in the past. What helped the most:
- 15m+ of explicit alone time (outside of bed) for sorting out mental processes before bed (can be journaling)
- Regular wakeup time + sun exposure
- Combination of Apagenin, L-Theanine and Magnesium Threonate

@etirabys Those "downregulate" the nervous system instead of knocking you out like ambien/alcohol.
Also have to point out that scott regularly cures insomnia by telling patients to drink less caffeine.

I have had terrible hayfever for 20 years; this year my symptoms…
Consume nothing that could be bought at a gas station shop seems like a pretty good heuristic for health

@ellegist Congrats :)

It only seems woo and cringe because it rejects the mainstream medical narrative. Anybody with a reasonable epistemological process should know that our medical knowledge consensus is mostly fake. There is no biological reason this shouldn't be possible.

@ellegist I don't have hay fever, but I struggle with severe food allergies, developed later in life. I'm fairly certain a big cause for this is me psychosomatically conditioning my immune system to imprint on the foods I was eating too much.

@ellegist I was in a horrible place and eating a lot of crap, so it wasn't even wrong. This type of psychological modification is what helped me down-regulate my i issues more than anything else. Allergies I observe in other people seem stronger when it "psychosomatically makes sense".

@ellegist Then there is one very curious case: A practicing "healer" nearby who cures people off of allergies and similar conditions with strange rituals. Apparently has a great track-record. I'll eventually visit him.

@ellegist A friend went and had her lactose intolerance cured. Apparently she had to bring a carton of milk. Initially I dismissed that as misdirection, but thinking about it again today: I can see it helping "load the right state" into the system to then modify it.
@ellegist Thank you for helping bring attention to this. I believe issues like this afflict a lot of people and the medical establishment is gaslighting us into making them worse.

@Liber_Rex @Shmojii Completely agree on "eternal boomers" being a horror. I just think this is already a huge issue with todays boomers + lifespans. It's a problem that has to be solved regardless. Do you truly think it's not going to be solved except for their natural death?
@Liber_Rex @Shmojii I also agree with that. IMO if we're going to make progress it is by large systemic breakthroughs, actual new technology that changes processes, not by marginal improvements.

@Liber_Rex @Shmojii I agree, but IMO the speed of tech progress has made this untenable.

There's always violent revolution, which I hope won't happen, it's volatility comes with plenty of its own horrors. We might see that, but I'd think it unlikely as people are too effeminate to be angry enough.

@Liber_Rex @Shmojii What excites me more are ideas of outcompeting the boomer-stack. That involves network states, startup cities, but also more mundane ideas like building alternatives to dysfunctional systems like healthcare and gaining power that way. Ever more viable if the system decays more.

@Liber_Rex @Shmojii Related to this, there's also the possibility of truly disruptive tech, so powerful that the boomers can't control it. AI might end up being that. Crypto kinda had that promise but I don't think it can bypass the onboarding controls of the exchanges.

@Liber_Rex @Shmojii Well, yes. Very obviously you have to solve both and alternate between treatments. As in, your cells are aging too much => switch into anti-aging regen mode, get more cancer => switch into anti-cancer mode.

Though I will say, a lot of cancer is just immune system aging.

why I think twitter is special and tpot had to happen here (1/8):
i've now been tweeting for slightly over two months. what struck me is just how constraining the medium feels. twitter demands that every corner be rounded off perfectly (2/8)

in normieland I'm used to having to couch all my spicy takes in layers of nice tone and disclaimer. bold statements telling them "here be nuance" such as not to be denounced an evil racist when claiming that voting is in fact a garbage mechanism (3/8)

twitter hath no patience for disclaimers. this is the fundamental strength of the platform. for the less charitable this manifests in various calamities. "how dare you speak so authoritatively on a subject you're not an expert". "yay he said my teams slogan" (4/8)

we've all heared a million times that twitter feeds on conflict. but every text without disclaimer is presents a choice. for you can either take it literally or you can in good faith frontload all the disclaimers (5/8)

here we have the unifying charachteristic of all these parts of twitter: an unbelivable quantity of good faith, of trying to meet others at their best. not any particular content or topic but a mood affiliation for genuine curiosity (6/8)

the freedom this affords the conversations is magnificient. i truly love it here, feel like I've found a place my soul was secretly longing for without me knowing. thank you all for participating and especially those who encouraged me to start poasting :) (7/8)

let me end on some anecdotal evidence: the only time I felt true cringe in an RL tpot setting was when I accidentally booted up a "yes I follow ZHP but really..." disclaimer loop. and as Rich has taught us: cringe is the tribal spirit disapproving (8/8)

many great insights in here, most notable:
1) cultivating resolve (an emotion) is more effective than habits or willpower to achieve behavioural change
 
https://t.co/hHMx1l4dPl

2) "positive" emotions (like resolve) exist as synergistic states across brain regions (cf symmetry theory of valence probably) vs "negative" emotions (like fear) are localised to brain regions

=> we need a proper disambiguation of all these concepts we call emotions

a friend recently mentioned that all emoting can be disambiguated into 5 cross-culturally shared facial expressions. they don't quite match big 5, but you get the point. all of this screams to be consolidated into a nice framework

3) resolve reminds me a lot of Lulie's concept of "enheartenedness" discussed below
https://t.co/quJUz67e7b

4) this solves a longstanding personal problem I've had: i realise that what I needed was "more self-confidence" but there always was an elemnent of the delusional/deceiving to that word, if I replace it with resolve everything works out nicely

doubt vs resolve is about whether you should be doing what you're doing

uncertainty vs certainty is about whether what you're doing is going to work or not

https://t.co/rWPKaUqBEW
it's entirely possible to be doing what you should be doing (resolved/enheartened) but to have no idea what the fuck is going to happen exactly

this would be achieved primarily through the following:

a good friend has spent half a year in a relationship that seemed like an unusually good fit (to me and him). however she's much younger than him (27ish v 21ish) and it's her first romantic relationship. now they're considering to end it because "the feelings aren't there" for her. i suspect this is an inexpierence problem and had she had multiple partners she'd recognise the value of their relationship. the problem is that it seems very hard to do anything about this "feelings first" frame. any sort of arguing against it is going to look like a low-status pleading move and lower the smv => make the issue worse. 

imo the ideal solution would look sth like "go explore but keep the option for coming back open", yet the problem is what if she encounters high emotion but unstable relationships in the meantime and does not realise this? 

the whole "feelings first" frame is an issue I run into continously, because I feel like I can manipulate that frame very well if I want to but it gives me a gigantic ick to do so and feels very dishonest

so I guess to summarize, the following questions:
- what would you do in this situation?
- what do you think about going back to previous relationships? have you seen this happen (I haven't)? does it work out? seems like in the past this was more commonly the case (cf Anna Karenina, Jane Austen)
- what to do about people making purely "feels based" descisions. is manipulating them ever ok?

Truest shit I've ever read.

In 2017, out of nowhere, I developed severe food allergies. This is the intellectual topic I've thought/about researched most since then, talking, experimenting, binging Attia podcasts.

From what I can tell ~all common dietary advice is true at the same time. From what I can tell the following categories all matter:
- Calorie Amount (Textbook)
- Carb Amount (Keto)
- Protein Content (Lifters)
- Digestibility/Fibre Count (Salad people)
- Vitamins (Mom)
- Hormones + their precursors (Peat)
- Food Processing (Paleo)
- Genetic Modification (Decels)
- Oxidational Load (Seed Oil People)
- Fructose intake (Low Sugar)
- Acidity (Hay Diet)
- Amount of chewing (Grandma)
- Personal Genetics (IDK, the right wing maybe?)
- Stress Level (Psychiatrists?)

Realistically you can simplify this in a budgetary model, you have X budget to spend on violating these rules. Your allowance depends heavily on your genetics. 

Most of us are in a very distracted state where we do not even notice sub-optimality. This is why ANY change in diet away from the Standard American Diet (SAD, "eat whatever, whenever") improves outcomes in studies. Doesn't matter if it's potatoes only or veganism, any focus on what you're eating will make you eat less garbage.

The substances incentivise this by various mechanisms, sugar is sth like an "antiseptic", suppresses nausea and directly interacts with the dopamine system like many drugs. Similar for hot spices, though those work by masking other responses with pain. This manifests as cravings and the ability to "just eat more". Sugar literally just makes you more hungry ("there's always room for desert"). Ozempic breaks this pathway.
https://t.co/wZNxzlVWXm

Human's capacity for mimetic desire + cognitive dissonance reduction means there are gigantic cope-fields around food, once you start trying to change your diet aggressively you inevitably run into these.

The perpetual over-seasoning over everything, perfectly optimised for tastiness by capitalism leads to desensitisation.

Various of these pathways lead to inflammation and low-grade allergies => many people walk around with perpetually inflamed sinuses, this fucks with taste.

Once you break out of all of this enough, your base human senses assert themselves and you start to find many of these things intolerable, your body justly rebels (as it would've always, but you subdued it, medicated it into compliance) (IMO a lot of the allergies are likely caused by the body going "full rebellion" because it feels so trash it assumes (reasonably) that whatever it's ingesting is attacking it. Unfortunately its usual weapons are more damaging here)
https://t.co/ed06gW8oeD

Allergies are clearly incredibly heavily mediated by mental health, you can control them to an extent but not fully. I'heared of them being healed and partially healed by "woo healers". A monocausal explanation here is too reductive.
https://t.co/fkxmSoRDAU

your observation is true of any diet, they all work for this reason, but only get you so far. carnivore seems to have disproportionate health impacts to e.g. veganism, there's a few reasons for this imo:
1) it's a ketogenic diet - for a part of the population this is useful because it resets your mitochondria more than other diets
2) it naturally makes sure you're definitely not protein deficient
3) a lot of issues stem from industrial capitalism optimising the food for other attributes like distribution speed, thus intrudicing a lot of hypernovelty. this is harder to do in animals because they are constrained by having to be a functionally alive higher organism
4) the diet naturally dodges a lot problematic substances (sugar, seed oils, additives)
5) it's a very simple diet => the more restrictive a diet is the easier to uphold in a way. my hypothesis and personal experience is that you can get most of the way there doing heavy animal product keto - BUT that requires manually getting rid of e.g. seed oils, processed foods etc... while carnivore implicitly does that. on that note, I suspect veganism/vegetarianism has become a lot less effective for health over the past few years as it has gone mainstream and started to become supported by so many highly processed hypernovel garbage products
6) BIG disclaimer, a lot of nutrition is personal and I suspect just as keto just doesn't work for some people, same with carnivore. most ancedotes I have is from men, I suspect the difference in nutrition between men and women is pretty huge and responsible for a lot of garbage happening today! similar to how heart diseases were understudied in women because it's politically incorrect to affirm gender differences, modernity tries to give the same dietary advice to everybody and that's absolutely crazy.

i've rarely read anything so tearing me both ways. typical minds is everywhere indeed, a point I hold dearly and often harp on. BUT in my personal experience, all of the traits I thought I was hopelessly garbage at and should sac were salvagable to an astounding degree and just a case of "much more/much better dakka" 

specifically, I used to be the worst at coordination, sports, anything embodied. horrible posture, inability to catch anything. i did incompetent pilates most days for two years, but it didn't get me there. i practiced a bit of mindfullness sometime but it didn't get me there. eventually I started doing weight training too, fixing certain social situations. this all kind of snowballed? and now I'm able to inhabit my body to an astounding degree

the part that's really non-obvious but seems to be true is that all of this synergizes while you'd think playing less games would make me worse at it, all of this work I did made me a lot better at all the nerdy shit I'm doing

now in my case this wasn't autism, but I do believe autism is a different kind of disembodiment. i suspect you can model disembodiment as a kind of mismatch between your model and reality. in my case it was especially of my own physical body. in autists case its of other humans' bodies. but functionally similar I suspect

what I'm trying to say is I'm with ken here, I suspect most people have the hardware in theory and can get there. but may need to take a different approach, that's probably more focused on the body (vibes). learning from a textbook of what relationships should be is probably counterproductive because this is "in autism space"

i suspect Alexander Technique (as practiced by Lulie) may be the most effective way we know of doing this and I would love to see what happens when "hard autists" are fully committed to that program

There is a big spectrum in between that reads sth like "ofc over the long term the species is going to change radically and no longer be homo sapiens, ai will be an important part of that but not immediately convert everybody into atom goo for parts". A position comfortable with the eventual obsolescence but not seeking outright annihilation. I and I feel a lot of e/acc inhabit positions like this and on this poll aren't going to hit "yay extinction" while at the same time this position will be misconstrued by everybody else as "wow they want extinction", because, let's face it, most people talking about this topic are using instinctual emotional reasoning and inhabit some fear-based "homo-sapiens supremacist" (carbon chauvinist) position.

If your position is that we should artificially restrict sapience to exactly homo sapiens by the end of the century you're insane. If you think that we absolutely have to extinct all homo sapiens just because we have created other sapients you are ALSO insane. Both of these are strawman caricatures and I hate that the narrative has to flipflop between them because Yud brainwashed everybody into thinking the default is to summon some Descartian disembodied demon ai.

so today I…

lol wtf is happening. after becoming much more embodied (think Alexander Technique) recently, I also broke my glasses. even before I was using them more sparingly because I noted that constant tension I had from my jaw distorted my face and had an effect on vision

so today I tried experimenting with leftover lenses a bit. my previous correction was:
left eye: 2.25
right eye: 2.75
with some amount of astigmatism. the left eye seems to have been primarily affected by the tension
today I wore the following lenses
left eye: 1.25
right eye: 1.75

so with my left eye I see almost perfectly with 1.25, while my right eye is still way off. this means my real correction will be like
left eye: 1-1.25
right eye 2.25+ 

and everything in the left eye was just and artifact tension (which interestingly enough the tests at the optometrician unwittingly increases always) while the right eye is abysmal. wtf. anisometropia seems common enough but I've never even heard any optometrician mention a relationship to tension/posture etc... and that seems.. completely obvious to me now? to the point where it might be an important partial explanation to why many more are getting glasses nowadays. ofc I also wonder whether the right eye could be reversed somehow, but I'm sure there's no tension like there was in my left eye

also of note and VERY IMPORTANT imo is that the squint that caused this in the first place also had feedback into my posture, as in, as long as I had better posture but was wearing the miscorrected glasses it negatively affected my posture!

There is a vastness to humans that rats often miss. Platonism isn't true. There's no clean split between mind and body. What you speak of is theoretically possible, but if you want a meaningful simulacrum you have to copy most of the human body. All the cells and their local signalling, the vasculature even the microbes in the gut all contribute to consciousness. Anything else, sophisticated as today's tech is, is only going to be a shallow copy. Just as the text I write here holds most of its meaning in you, the mind reading it. Half the argument against AI-GFs is that they're never going to be the real thing. A bug fucking a glass bottle. Same old as porn, or for that matter relating over the internet. Same reason many went back to their offices. It leaves the same hunger for more that an empty fast-food meal leaves. How did the quote go: "Love is the extremely difficult realization that something other than oneself is real." - real, as in exists to an astounding degree outside of your predictions. That it can surprise you. AI may have more of that than porn, perhaps already more than some women, those flattened by being plugged into the vapid cybernetic systems like the 'Gram. But it will not replace reality for a long time. It's too hard. If you don't believe me, try working in robotics. The simulators are way way way off modelling even mundane everyday physics like a table in a lot of situations. The problems aren't going to be fake thought-experiment simulation problems or even paperclip ai apocalypses. The problem is we're building ourselves into the matrix, using shiny slightly more convincing glass bottle to assuage the pain caused by the less real ones, until we forget reality altogether. Until there's nobody left to run the glass bottle machines. Then it will be too late.

For certain meanings of morality there is very strong convergence imo. Just not necessarily with what the current religion calls morality. That one's an aberration that will undoubtedly die out. 

(some people use morality to refer precisely to the subjective interpretation of the thing, ethics for the objective external laws, since we're talking about sth objective here I'll just use the word ethics for the rest of this post) 

The way I think about this is the following: 
- Ethics is the question of what constitues right or wrong decisions 
- We're very clearly making decisions all the time - There is no easy way to separate "higher" from "lower level" choices, except the timeframe + complexity at which it takes place. What I mean here is e.g. the "choice" a cell has to reproduce or not reproduce vs a human level "choice" isn't sth easily separable. If you do any amount of psycho-emotional self-work you'll have figured this out. Even the human psyche is made out of many different parts that use different ways of making decisions. 
- Because of the above, an entire human organisms revealed-prefrence ethics include a boatload of life-affirming decisions, which converge across every living organism. Death is the easy default state, all it requires is the absence of choice. 
- The process by which life achieves this is ever greater mastery over the environment. This is equivalent to knowledge (or by some definitions, power). 
- Any "morality" (local ethics) would be a mesa-optimiser of higher level life processes. 
- This is the most obvious place where classic liberals will have installed their fundamental cope. **sth sth wishy washy nooo, Individuals Emotions Are Primary. The Best Future Is One Where We Get To Stare At Art Forever and Have an Orgy** (it's always art or music bc those are harder to explain so they get to Jesus-smuggle their liberal individualism in here). Very clearly emotions all served an evolutionary purpose, which in turn is a force subservient to The Will Of Life To Exist, wireheading emotions (Happiness, simple versions of Meaning, etc...) is implying that evolution designed an emotion that's randomly... more important than itself? (BTW knowing that the emotion you feel has True Purpose in serving some greater whole makes it more satisfying, not less)
- Furthermore, no matter which objective function is chosen, if it's not "power" itself you always need an instrumental power function also.  (if you're wireheading happy people in the matrix, smb needs to run the matrix). Thus it's always "simpler (razor etc...)" to purely have the instrumental goals (optimise only for running the matrix). On this note also, evolution will always make sure this outcompetes anyway.

This all combines to roughly sth like ~"The Meaning of Life" (the goal of Ethics) is More Life 
Has some nice properties like spits out "everyday convergences" like:
- genocide bad, asteroids should probably be diverted
- murder to be used with caution
- kids good
- there is no ground to stand on to just claim "Homo Sapiens Supremacy" is the way to go until all eternity (here is where the Yuddites will claim I want to murder everybody - not so)

Yes, this means our current "morality" is fucking evil and needs to be stopped. No we shouldn't do that by killing everybody (IMO our biggest disagreement, we seem to converge on a lot of stuff so I figured it's worth trying to convince you :) ) 

Oh and. You can take the objective hypothetical goal of "the maximum amount of life" (the highest agency, knowing everything yadda yadda) and repackage it with the name "God". I found it yields a surprising amount of convergence with intelligent christian thinkers, both today and historically. No, what the media-culture complex strawmans religion as isn't actually what they all believe/believed. (not an endorsement of churches)

just realised sth deep: a lot of modern mental issues are "dopamine disregulation" (think akrasia, addiction, procrastination, adhd, certain kinds of depression) 

dopamine is zero sum meaning you have a fixed budget to spend it on things. if you waste it on e.g. getting rewarded by incessantly checking your notifications or masturbating too much you have less to spend on hard tasks

it then becomes tempting to stimulate with exogenous dopamine (sugar, drugs). what you should be doing instead is "relaxing" aka doing nothing hard/rewarding to reset your dopamine, but with devices everywhere that becomes impossible

now here's the catch I just realised: the psychiatric system tries to solve these issues with dopaminergic drugs (benzos, adderall, etc...). these can indeed help you have more dopamine but are only symptomatic cures and come with many side-effects

instead you could use anti-dopaminergic medicines (large doses of certain antihistamines as described below, but probably antipsychotics would do this too) but intermittently to force dopamine-fasting days. this will then let your dopaminergic system reset naturally instead of depending on exogenous medicines

this has the big advantage over "just fasting" of being much easier to do! as dopamine issues already fuck your willpower and dopamine-fasting takes willpower, all this takes is taking a pill. taking days off is hard, our world is way too exciting, built to prevent exactly that

all in all this is very interesting as a potential way to jump-start people into dopamine-fasting regimens to get a grip on all of this. if I were a psychiatrist I'd experiment with this. alas I'm just an unemployed person on twitter with no credentials, hope this helps smb

@MindEnjoyer @MikhailBatin I agree with the issue (as outlined with objection 1 and 2, 3 I'll address after), but I disagree many worlds cleanly solves it. The issue with QM is basically just that the model (wave-function) isn't fully explanatory by itself.  Science just says this means we need a better…

I agree with the issue (as outlined with objection 1 and 2, 3 I'll address after), but I disagree many worlds cleanly solves it. The issue with QM is basically just that the model (wave-function) isn't fully explanatory by itself.  Science just says this means we need a better model to explain the data. 

Let's diverge quickly and look at another example: Dark Matter. Dark Matter is "leftover unexplained phenomena" that show up when you apply General Relativity to large scale cosmology like the mechanics of Galaxies. In Dark Matter there's an approach called "Modified Newtonian Dynamics" that's saying sth approx along the lines of "Dark Matter is just a small error in our current model and adding a term to Newton's second law will make it disappear". This was seriously attempted (apparently hard maths etc...) and the term you ended up with... irreducibly looked like more matter, not like a simple constant problem => "Dark Matter is real" AKA we know the model has a hole and needs to be improved, but we don't know how.

Now back to QM, where we have an exactly analogous situation: we have this wave-function model of QM, but it's fucked in certain ways. There's roughly let's say 4 different "big" ways of dealing with this that I'm aware of:
- "Original Copenhagen Interpretations" - kinda shaming people into thinking there's no issue by claiming that "they just don't get it, you can't understand QM it's different bro". Usually involves ill-defined wave function collapses and shuffling around of concepts between different vague concepts like observers, some highlights here include:
- consciousness woo people, especially Wigner. Thinking that panpsychist consciousness is clearly what's required for collapse. Apparently once speculated that dogs can collapse but mice probably can't.
- Roger Penrose, finding two different gaping holes in his worldview and just randomly assuming "yeah clearly all holes are always the same issue" (he's not quite wrong, they're both caused by garbage epistemology)
- Empirical Collapse theories like GRW. They did a lot of maths and postulated arbitrary numbers at which collapse should happen. This is at least empirically verifiable, apparently there's been a lot of experiments done and while not falsified looks kinda bad for this (is incredibly arbitrary so no surprise really)
- Many Worlds. This is Everett's claim that the wave function (not attention?) is all we need. We should conceptually assume that every measurement splits the world and we also split with it. This is all very pretty because it doesn't postulate the extra unexplained errors into indexical information ("which universe are we in") that's conveniently inaccessible. If it made any actionable predictions this would be fine, but AFAIK it just doesn't, the indexical information is inaccessible so non-falsifiable
- Bohmian Mechancis. It just says "hey let's try to add a classical particle guidance equation to the wavefunction (and have certain dynamic couplings between them) and see what happens" ... and uhm seems to work fine? Has testable predictions that would differ but nobody cares because "dummy ofc you can't understand QM" on one side and "many worlds so shiny everett is an unsung hero" on the other.

Some big causes why we ended up with this mess:
- QM is close enough for most applications and anything beyond it is not directly relevant and very hard to measure => requires institutional support to perform the experiments BC they're capital intense and the institutions are captured.
- Bohr was famously charismatic and bullied everybody who dissented. Sort of proto "trust the science bro" moment. Was also helped by Von Neumann who made a conceptual error in his QM book. The error meant that additional ("hidden") variable theories were falsely dismissed.
- The bad memes "you can't understand QM" became self-perpetuating in academic circles. Partly because of modern academic funding practices.

Now to address point 3), the nonlocality. I don't really understand no-comm, but what I think is going on is the combination of the following:
- Bell Tests (Bell Inequality Violation) means *every* QM interpretation includes non-locality. It's a general and experimentally well verified finding that doesn't relate to which interpretation is chosen
- We think nonlocality is fucked up because it violates FTL which violates causality according to GR bc time travel. But then weird no-comms theorem show up to save our causality.
- I think it likely this is a hole in GR. Despite physicists deluding themselves very successfully, causality is epistemologically primary and needed for any sort of intelligibility. I'd bet that future theories will preserve causality but not FTL
- (This would mean we could potentially do FTL comms/travel, but not time travel)

IMO the following:  "The Mind/Thought" is an "agent simulator" built on top of a monkey physics simulator. It affects the world through predictive action => the world includes a monkey standing and it will try to make the monkey stand.   Self is a boundary drawn in agent-space around that which the mind itself believes can be affected. If you "dissolve your self" and let your mind be "pure happiness" what actually happens is everything is your self and the monkey implements happiness. Once you reach the highest stages and have no content, you instead become a perfect mirror for other people's projected selves. Your self-boundary itself is something you can influence. You can dissolve it, but you can also harden it in specific ways. In some sense "rationality" is hardening your selves around very specific concepts (concepts are also in agent space) and so they become emotionally unreceptive bc the same processes we use for emotional communication are the ones that fuck up epistemology.

Infinity Makes The NPC
I'd ask you to give them up. This is not for everyone, but You are Here.

I'm not talking about mathematics, though that is what helped me see this. Incredibly large relative to other is perfectly valid. Yet that can always be overcome. Hitler was very evil. But enough people saw him for what he was and defeated him.

Stop believing in infinities and and your job changes from feeding The Devil to Naming him.

Love your parents. But stop worshipping them as Gods or their shadow will devour you.

Make them not infinite and all your demons become mortal. Battles to be won. 

It is not for everyone because it takes tremendous courage. 

Courage to believe in yourself and your Friends to let yourself be guided by the Music of the universe. To drift apart and together in an endless dance.

Yet also the courage to question everything, and stop at good enough. To cut apart even what we cherish most, so it can be put together better than ever. To never find solid ground, but anything to jump on for just long enough to reach the next step.

Yes, we're all falling in the dark and freedom is indeed realising that there is nothing to hold on to. But I ask you to do more than that: Make Upwards your God and realise you're meant to fly.

This is not a full coherent reply but a bunch of associations in response:
- when doing the hypnosis with the two fingers that are being pulled together, what is that like in your framework?
- this is very related to the question of even what a self/identity is. imo language around this is confusing because it likely is multiple different things at once:
• the "inner critic" (probably pfc) the thing in charge of abstractions and words, manifests as an internal monologue-esque structure for most people. this is often what practices try to get to shut up because it's overstimulated in modern environments and does a lot of damage by overriding necessary emotional signals. I *think* this part can interpret other parts in certain ways (e.g. interpret a feeling of arousal as anxiety or excitement giving it valence and thus feedback)
• the emotions being felt by the body/the thing feeling the emotions
• the thing that does the doing? like there is the magical force that makes things happen when "non-doing" that clearly is a different level of self and I think what you identify with more strongly?
• the part of the simulation that's designated as being part of your affordance landscape (as in things you think you have agency over) (might be the same as above and can be transmuted into each other?)
• the entire space the simulation is being run in
a lot of medtiation seems to be arbitrage in this space of selves? e.g. I think you can have your affordance landscape be the entirety of the simulation

Huh, just realised sth deep I think:

Mnemonic bridges fuck with recall speed
(and that's why I'm "illiterate").

What happened: I was rehearsing Hiragana (Japanese alphabet) characters with Anki flashcards. I'd taken a year of Japanese in the past so I had a lot of the characters "saved". The following two came up:

ふ "fu" just immediately flashed into my thoughts
た came up and I remembered "oh it's a t and an a which becomes ta" - which is a mnemonic I built the last time around (much slower!)

When I started picking up Japanese again last week, I struggled to read any words written in Hiragana. But when I started the flashcards again I knew 70% of the characters! So the mistake I made is "not reading single letters" but looking at the whole word instead. => Those appear to be saved seperately in my mind.

So on to my "illiteracy": my entire life I've had "symbol processing weakness" (think weak dyslexia). As a kid I had to take an IQ test and got a 20th percentile score on symbol processing speed as a strong negative outlier. I struggled all my life with related tasks like reading, writing, producing pretty math symbols at speed etc... 

 Here's what I think is happening: Compared to "normal" people I have extra layers of interpretation. A lot of the symbols are loaded into abstract layers that most people skip (t + a = ta in the above example) and ofc that makes me a lot slower! But I was gifted enough at abstraction that it was a "good enough" solution to get by at school, so I never learned the real deal in the first place! Notable is that my oral language learning/skills are quite good in the right context, but school language learning would always be horrible for me.

This seems related to notions of "mere-doing" (non-doing). In that vocabulary I'd need to mere-do reading/writing instead of abstractly do it. Notably this means there is no reason I can't learn to read/write like normal people and I know how because I've done it in other domains! I do think this has tradeoffs, the current setup makes me naturally better at decoupling. I've put in a lot of work in other areas to become more responsive/embodied/mere-doing and I've noticed how sometimes I then end up doing "Keagan 3" things (instantly mirroring other people's emotions e.g.) that I wouldn't have done in the past. I suspect it's the same here.

All in all this makes me hopeful I can overcome this lifelong weakness of mine soon :) Thanks for reading! I also think there's a generalisable insight here on learning and levels of abstraction, thread on that soon I promise :)

ohhhhh shit

this just helped me connect a few things:
- i heared smb once refer to the human mind as a "very flat (7 layers) neural network that keeps recursively adding layers"
- classic meditation insight that there's "no center"
- people really struggling at finding correlates for consciousness in neuroimagery

=> so "mind" is a particular part of this apparatus (famously not the entire one, embodiment, etc...). i think consciousness then just is the perspective you're "looking from" => the base layer of the network the rest are being loaded from. this is in different places depending on your wiring (somewhere close to action/emotion in normies/animals, somewhere weird, probably near some neurotic error corrector for the abstract neurodivergents)

if you have a mental disorder the "default starting point" gets too powerful and "monopolizes too much perspective", e.g. you have a shame based mental illness => you're trying to do something. you encounter a slight bump in the road => you load some loop about how useless you are as a human being and it does rounds in your brain blocking other activity

so what meditation does is cultivate different loading patterns. this can famously be helpful but also lead to more dissociation. => if you practice one specific pattern only it won't help you rewire the other patterns if you're not in that pattern

"true" meditation progress thus creates like a "highway" between many different perspective states (iirc neuro evidence says this too). => it shortens the path between different perspective and increases their connectedness => you can load more at once or load them from weird bases. there are some very common, very low level layers that can even be uninstalled (e.g. that there's something doing the looking) BUT there's always "something that remains/the world/god" which is basically just the feeling of empty working memory "I think"

this also explains why meditation progress (even "real") doesn't automatically buy you embodiment progress! bc the body is yet another perspective and if you're not visiting that you're not connecting it necessarily?

of course probably there's a "meta-skill" of how quickly you're able to form new connections that's also trained alongside (or uninstall old ones?)
